items.title: Ukraine reports Russian attacks and battlefield clashes despite ceasefire
items.summary: Ukraine and ‌Russia agreed on May 8 to a ​ceasefire from May 9 to May 11 ⁠as part of a U.S.-led push for peace under President Donald Trump to end more than four years ‌of war since Russia’s full-scale invasion in 2022

items.title: At least six people found dead in boxcar in Laredo, Texas, police say
items.summary: Past instances ‌of multiple deaths in trains and tractor trailers not far from the U.S.-Mexico border have involved migrants

items.title: Asian shares mixed, oil jumps 4% after Donald Trump rejects Iran's response to ceasefire proposal

items.title: Eyeing migrant returns, EU pushes to revive Syria ties

items.title: Thailand's former PM Thaksin released from prison
items.summary: The 76-year-old telecoms billionaire served eight months of a one-year prison sentence for corruption and will be required to wear an electronic monitor during his four-month probation period

items.title: Asia braces for second wave of energy shocks from Iran war
items.summary: Governments made difficult trade-offs: saving power at the risk of slowing businesses, prioritising gas for households at the risk of fertiliser production and dipping into energy stockpiles for temporary relief

items.title: Donald Trump eyes deal in Beijing as China gauges U.S. ‘decline’
items.summary: Washington and Beijing announce May 13-15 visit of U.S. President to China with trade, technology, and Taiwan on the agenda

items.summary: Pakistan PM Shehbaz Sharif claimed that Pakistani armed forces gave a "historic and befitting response" to the enemy, he said while addressing a ceremony on May 10, 2026, to mark the first anniversary of 'Marka-e-Haq'

items.title: Americans from virus-hit ship will not necessarily be quarantined: official
items.summary: The U.S. passengers, all of whom are asymptomatic, will be taken to a specialised centre in the rural state of Nebraska, but will not necessarily be quarantined there, says Mr. Bhattacharya

items.title: Iran war ‘not over,’ uranium must be removed: Netanyahu
items.summary: In an interview aired May 10, Donald Trump said Iran was “militarily defeated” and he insisted the uranium could be removed “whenever we want”

items.summary: The two were reported missing May 2 after participating in African Lion, an annual multinational military exercise held in Morocco

items.summary: The visit comes in the midst of a prolonged U.S.-Israel-Iran war causing a global energy crisis and the increasing tensions between the two countries over a host of issues, including Taiwan

items.title: Turkish Airlines jet catches fire while landing at Nepal’s main airport; all passengers safe
items.summary: Several planes bound for Kathmandu were put on hold while officials were investigating and attempting to clear the only runway available at the airport

items.title: Under-threat British PM Starmer to attempt reset after disastrous polls

items.title: Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi transferred to a Tehran hospital, her foundation says
items.summary: Narges Mohammadi’s transfer comes after days of pleading by her family and others who described her condition as critical. Her foundation said she has been granted a prison sentence suspension on bail

items.title: Iran responds to U.S. ceasefire proposal but Trump rejects it as ‘unacceptable’
items.summary: Iran seeks to end the war on all fronts, including in Lebanon, where Israel is fighting the Iranian-backed Hezbollah militant group, and to ensure the security of shipping, state TV said

items.title: Plane with U.K. citizens from hantavirus ship lands in Manchester
items.summary: A British official said 20 of them had flown in on the special flight to Manchester; the other two did not live in Britain and would be travelling to their countries of residence

items.title: Syria sets first cabinet reshuffle since Assad ouster, state media reports
items.summary: The reshuffle ‌is the first since the ouster of former President Bashar al-Assad and comes around ⁠a year and a half into the five-year transitional period set ‌out in Syria’s constitutional declaration

items.title: Iran-Israel war Highlights: Iran sends its response to U.S. proposal aimed at ending the war, says IRNA
items.summary: Iran's head of military meets Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, says state TV; Iran on May 9 said it was still reviewing the United States’ proposal linked to ongoing negotiations over the West Asia conflict

items.title: Militia kill at least 69 in DR Congo: local, security sources
items.summary: Two ethnic groups — the Hema and the Lendu — have been locked in a long-running violent conflict in Ituri, a gold-rich province that borders Uganda and South Sudan

items.title: 15 security officials killed in militant attack in northwest Pakistan
items.summary: The impact of the explosion led to the collapse of the roofs of several houses in the vicinity. The police post building was also razed to the ground, and several security personnel were buried under the rubble

items.title: India, Trinidad and Tobago sign eight MoUs during Jaishankar visit
items.summary: Agreements on tourism, healthcare, infrastructure and Ayurveda signed during External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar’s two-day visit, as both sides review progress on initiatives announced during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit last year

items.title: Explosion at coal mine in Colombia, at least four trapped
items.summary: The explosion happened on Saturday (May 9) afternoon at the Las Quintas mine in Pueblo Viejo, a village in Cucunuba municipality, said Jorge Emilio Rey, governor of the department of Cundinamarca.

items.title: Bangladesh’s HC rejects detained Hindu monk’s bail petition
items.summary: Chattogram Divisional Speedy Trial Tribunal on January 19 indicted Das, a former ISKCON leader, and 38 others in connection with the death of the lawyer in Chattogram and opened a trial against him.

items.title: U.K.'s Starmer says his government is a ten-year project despite calls to quit
items.summary: We got a real kicking from the voters, there’s no escaping that, says Bridget Phillipson, the Education Minister.

items.title: Non-binary Indian migrant elected member of Scottish Parliament
items.summary: “As a queer Tamil immigrant, I am standing to be an MSP to bring a politics of care and compassion to Holyrood, to fight for the radical change our working-class and marginalised communities need," says Manivannan in his poll pitch for the Scottish Greens

items.title: Indonesian rescuers find hikers' bodies after volcanic eruption
items.summary: Their bodies were found not far from where the remains of an Indonesian hiker had been found a day earlier, says National Disaster Mitigation Agency spokesman Abdul Muhari in a statement

items.title: Iran ceasefire tested as cargo ship catches fire after being hit off Qatar's coast

items.title: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill three, medics say, testing fragile ceasefire
items.summary: Medics said an air strike ‌killed one person in the Maghazi refugee camp in ‌the Gaza Strip, while ‌another ⁠killed the head of the ⁠criminal police force in Khan Younis, Wessam Abdel-Hadi, and his aide, according to ​Gaza’s Hamas-run ‌Interior Ministry

items.title: Twelve Pakistani police officers killed in car bombing and shootout
items.summary: A militant alliance known as the Ittehad-ul-Mujahideen claimed responsibility for the attack

items.title: EAM Jaishankar hands over India-assisted agro-processing facility in South Trinidad
items.summary: S. Jaishankar arrived in Port of Spain from Paramaribo on May 8 on the concluding leg of his three-nation tour of Jamaica, Suriname, and Trinidad and Tobago, aimed at deepening India’s engagement with the Caribbean nations

items.title: Dozens killed in fresh attacks in central Mali: local, security sources
items.summary: Attacks on May 8 were claimed by the Al-Qaeda-linked Group for the Support of Islam and Muslims (JNIM), who had already killed at least 30 people in attacks on villages on May 6

items.title: Putin says Ukraine war winding down, blasts West for backing Kyiv
items.summary: The Russian President said he was ready to meet Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy in a third country only once all conditions for a potential peace agreement were settled

items.summary: The report said expectations had grown that Tehran would issue a response after U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio indicated that Washington was expecting an answer from Iran

items.title: Rubio defends new U.S. sanctions on Cuba, targeting military-run conglomerate GAESA
items.summary: Established in the 1990s under military control, GAESA was the Cuban Armed Forces' strategic response to the economic collapse that followed the Soviet Union's fall and the tightening of U.S. sanctions in place at the time

items.title: UN mission in Democratic Republic of Congo warns of wave of attacks in east
items.summary: The mineral-rich eastern DRC has been plagued for three decades by conflict involving various armed groups, militia and army troops

items.title: Israeli drone strikes near Beirut kill four; southern airstrikes claim at least 13 lives
items.summary: The Israeli military said Hezbollah fired explosive drones into Israel near the border with Lebanon adding that three soldiers were wounded, one of them seriously, in one of the attacks

items.title: Plane hits and kills pedestrian during takeoff at Denver airport
items.summary: "Frontier Flight 4345 reported striking a pedestrian during takeoff" at around 11:19 p.m. on May 9, says Denver International Airport in a statement

items.title: Magyar sworn in as Hungary’s prime minister on promises of change
items.summary: But any honeymoon for the 45-year-old ​leader may be short-lived, with ‌the clock ticking to secure billions of euros in suspended European Union funding needed to kick-start the economy and shore up strained public finances

items.title: Army is fighting 'aggressive' NATO-backed force: Putin  in Victory Day speech
items.summary: The parade was vastly scaled back compared to previous years, with no military hardware on display for the first time in nearly two decades and only a handful of foreign dignitaries in attendance

items.title: U.K. PM Starmer turns to Labour Party grandees after tough local election verdict
items.summary: On the appointment of the two Labour Party grandees, Mr. Starmer said that as Britain’s longest-serving chancellor, former PM Gordon Brown is well placed to work with international allies to boost the country’s security and resilience

items.title: Indonesian rescuers find 1 body after volcano eruption as search continues for 2 more
items.summary: Rescuers on May 9, recovered the body of an Indonesian woman who was caught in a volcanic eruption at Mount Dukono on Indonesia’s remote island of Halmahera a day earlier, as search operations continued to find the bodies of two Singaporeans

items.title: WHO chief arrives in Spain to coordinate hantavirus ship evacuation
items.summary: The only hantavirus strain that can transmit from person to person — Andes virus — has been confirmed among those who have tested positive, fuelling international concern

items.title: Israel to release two Gaza flotilla activists ahead of deportation: rights group
items.summary: After over a week of Israeli detention, Saif and Avila are said to be released.

items.summary: Amidst controversy about billionaire patronage, Indians arrived at the Met Gala with art and history on their side

items.summary: The couple was found dead in their Abbotsford residence in May 2022

items.title: Australia's far-right party wins first lower house seat
items.summary: The ⁠result is in line with a surge of electoral support for far-right populist parties globally. ‌Britain's ruling Labour party this week suffered a widespread loss ‌of seats at council elections

items.title: Pakistan security forces kill 5 terrorists in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
items.summary: Four terrorists belonging to the banned Teh­reek-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP) were killed in the military engagement in the Tank district, the statement said

items.title: Labour loses power in Wales for first time since 1999 devolution
items.summary: Wales waves good-bye to Labour for first time since 1999 as the nationalist Plaid Cymru’s leader Rhun ap Iorwerth wins the election echoing its need for a ‘change of leadership’

items.title: Tehran could withstand blockade for four months, CIA report shows, as fighting flares
items.summary: Recent days have seen the biggest ‌flare-ups in fighting in and around the Strait of Hormuz since a ceasefire began a month ago, and the United Arab Emirates came under renewed attack on Friday (May 8, 2026)

items.title: Handwriting on new Epstein note matches one found after his death, say experts
items.summary: “They are written by the same person,” said Bart Baggett, who founded the forensic analysis firm Handwriting Experts Inc. and has testified in court as an expert witness more than 130 times
